scope: chrome-only — sub-interface internals out of scope

items.name: Candidate dumps a LOBBY PPM via screen-switch (sub-palette 2)
items.spec: Extend SILENCER_DUMP_SCREEN to handle 'lobby' and write a 640x480 P6 PPM at /tmp/sdl3_dump/screen_00.ppm under sub-palette 2. All six prior screen pathways unchanged.

items.name: Bank 7 idx 1 panel chrome renders correctly
items.spec: The bank-7 idx-1 lobby panel sprite renders fullscreen at (0,0). All chrome elements (corner LED ornaments, sub-interface frame borders, header bar separator) visible and structurally matching the reference. Distinct from CONTROLS' bank-7 idx-7 and LOBBYCONNECT's bank-7 idx-2.

items.name: Header bar correct (Silencer + version + map name + Go Back)
items.spec: Header at top of screen: 'Silencer' overlay at (15, 32) using textbank 135 / textwidth 11 / effectcolor 152; 'v.<version>' overlay at (115, 39) using textbank 133 / textwidth 6 / effectcolor 189; map name overlay at (180, 32) (empty on dump); B156x21 'Go Back' button at (473, 29). Per docs/design/screen-lobby.md.

items.name: Visual equivalence (chrome scope)
items.spec: Final gate, scoped to CHROME. The bank-7-idx-1 panel sprite + header bar + B156x21 Go Back button match the reference pixel-coincident in their respective regions. Sub-interface interior content (agency icons in CharacterInterface, planet/game-list in GameSelectInterface, chat scrollback in ChatInterface) is non-gated — diff budget is generous (~5-15%) because those interiors are runtime-data-driven and reserved for future per-sub-interface Ralphs.
